notes: A single no-op turn in ONE live aider REPL session launched against a specific main model: the user sends one short prompt, the turn completes, and the dashboard shows the launch model (ModelName != 'unknown') with a populated ContextWindow. THE MODEL/CONTEXT ASSERTION IS NOT EXPRESSIBLE IN THIS DSL: the events.jsonl lifecycle stream the validator reads carries no model_name/context_window field (the Phase struct has phase/expected_state/kind/max_delay_ms/duration_at_least_ms/same_session_as/new_session/invariants and nothing model-related). So this spec asserts only the BASIC-TURN LIFECYCLE SKELETON on ONE session — ready→working→ready on the SAME session_id, no waiting ever, a single process_exited at teardown. The two user-observable signals (ModelName == launch model, not 'unknown'; ContextWindow == the model's published window) are asserted out-of-band: at the metrics-level summary.model_name in the .replay.json.golden (diffed by replay-fixtures) plus the aider parser unit tests for the `> Model:`/`> Main model:` blockquote (parser_test.go) — NOT this file. WHY aider populates both signals: on the first assistant block aider writes a `> Model: <name> with <edit format>` blockquote to .aider.chat.history.md; the parser's modelRE (parser.go:66) captures the name, runs it through tailer.NormalizeModelName, and emits a Skip event carrying ModelName, which applyModelMetadata (tailer_metrics.go:110) stamps onto SessionMetrics.ModelName — so ModelName is set from turn one and is never 'unknown' for a model aider names. ContextWindow: aider's markdown transcript carries NO context-window field (unlike codex/claudecode), so the parser never sets an override; ContextWindow is derived ENTIRELY from the capacity manager's LiteLLM alias-table fallback (ComputeContextUtilization → capacityMgr.GetModelCapacity(modelName).ContextWindow, tailer_metrics.go:453) — the third resolution path in the scenario. For a LiteLLM-known launch model that yields the published window (ContextWindowUnknown=false); for a model absent from LiteLLM the window resolves to 0 / ContextWindowUnknown=true while ModelName stays correct (assessment caveat 1). Pass a known model via IRRLICHT_AIDER_MODEL so ContextWindow resolves deterministically. SESSION IDENTITY (aider-specific, differs from pi/codex): aider stores its transcript as .aider.chat.history.md in the cwd (FilesUnderCWD), so the daemon keys the WHOLE session by proc-<PID> — there is NO separate transcript-UUID rollout session and no handoff/reconcile phase. Every event (the state arc, pid_discovered, process_exited) lands on the one proc-<PID> row, so turn_start, turn_end, pid_bind, and teardown all pin to session_birth via same_session_as. LAZY TRANSCRIPT: .aider.chat.history.md materializes only once the first user input lands, so the live REPL is observable from launch as the proc-<PID> pre-session born ready (the process scanner) before the per-cwd transcript appears — session_birth, which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch', matches that pre-session born ready, so it is anchored to start UNPINNED (a same_session_as on the transient pre-session would let the matcher steal session identity from the short-lived proc-<PID> birth and cascade failures). AIDER TURN-END IS SYNTHESIZED, NOT MARKED: the markdown transcript has no turn-end marker — a `> Tokens:` line closes one model CALL (assistant_message), not the turn. End-of-turn is synthesized OUT-OF-BAND by IdleFlush after aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter (~1.5s) quiet, clamped to the SessionDetector's ~5s working-refresh poll, so the EFFECTIVE working→ready latency after the turn's last line is ~5s. The recipe therefore ends with a trailing sleep ≥6s so the turn's working→ready flip fires (IdleFlush) AND is observed (next poll) before daemon shutdown. WHY interactive (not headless `aider --message`): headless aider is single-shot and exits after one reply — on a short run the process can exit before the daemon classifies the working→ready settle and before the PID scanner ticks, truncating turn_end/teardown; the interactive driver keeps the REPL alive across the turn, then kills the tmux session once at the end → a SINGLE clean process_exited. SINGLE SESSION: exactly one proc-<PID> session_id and one process_exited. HOW runs differ: the concrete model value is environment-dependent (set IRRLICHT_AIDER_MODEL for a deterministic, LiteLLM-known name; otherwise issue #440 — assert presence, not a hardcoded string); exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, PID, and sub-second timing vary; the working→ready edge lands with ~5s effective idle-flush latency (a timing delay, not a correctness gap). Stable structurally: one ready→working→ready arc on ONE proc-<PID> session, a single process_exited, no waiting, and a final ready state — plus (golden-level, not here) a non-empty summary.model_name == the launch model and a populated ContextWindow. NOTE: this spec is the DOCUMENTED TARGET — validated when aider auth is available in-env (no provider key present at authoring time; headless aider hangs on OpenRouter OAuth).

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch (the proc-<PID> pre-session; the lazy transcript has not materialized yet)
The prompt → working on the launch model (binds the live aider session; the pre-session never enters working)

The turn ends → back to ready (IdleFlush synthesizes turn-end after the prompt goes quiet); state never entered waiting. After this point SessionMetrics.ModelName is the normalized launch model (not 'unknown') and ContextWindow is populated via the LiteLLM alias-table fallback — asserted at summary.model_name in the golden, not in this DSL
PID bound to the live aider session
REPL torn down once after the turn → a single process_exited on the session; state immediately before teardown is ready
